You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 87153ae1).

## Issue to Resolve

Formatting enum class with member definition and missing trailing commas causes rule violations on first run
## Expected Behavior
<!---Tell us what should happen. -->
<!-- If relevant, provide a link to the relevant section in the [Kotlin Coding Conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) or [Android Kotlin Style Guide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide).  -->

<!-- Note: Ktlint should not produce any code which conflict with IntelliJ default code formatting. So please verify that your expectation is accepted by IntelliJ default code formatting. -->

When formatting and validating the following enum class, the ktlint command should exit successfully (status `0`):

```kotlin
enum class TestEnum() {
    A,
    B;

    companion object {
        const val A_NUMBER = 1L
    }
}
```

## Observed Behavior
<!---Tell us what happens instead of the expected behavior -->
<!--- Provide the exact command which was executed but please -->
<!--- ensure that the flag "--log-level=debug" is added to the -->
<!--- command as well. Provide the output of this command. -->

When running ktlint on the above enum class, it is formatted correctly, but the run fails with rule violations (exit status `1`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format 
17:25:00.694 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:1:1: Missing trailing comma before "}" (trailing-comma-on-declaration-site)
/home/karl/tasks/ktlint-issue-example/TestEnum.kt:5:4: Unnecessary semicolon (no-semi)

Summary error count (descending) by rule:
  no-semi: 1
  trailing-comma-on-declaration-site: 1
Exit status: 1 
```

I note that the line number for the `no-semi` violation is the line number after formatting.

A second run on the (now properly formatted) code runs correctly (exit status `0`):
```bash
$ ktlint --format
17:25:05.397 [main] INFO com.pinterest.ktlint.internal.KtlintCommandLine - Enable default patterns [**/*.kt, **/*.kts]
Exit status: 0
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Run command on above enum class:
```ktlint --format```

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.48.1
* Operating System and version: Arch Linux

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I have the same issue

See release notes of 0.48.0:
> * The default value for trailing comma's on call site is changed to `true` unless the `android codestyle` is enabled. Note that KtLint from a consistency viewpoint *enforces* the trailing comma on call site while default IntelliJ IDEA formatting only *allows* the trailing comma but leaves it up to the developer's discretion. ([#1670](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1670))

@paul-dingemans I don't quite follow. My issue is that after doing formatting, ktlint reports invalid  `no-semi` and `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` rule violations. A subsequent run of ktlint (correctly) passes the same code.

Ah now I see. Although it is not visual in the textual representation of the code, the trailing comma is not inserted at the correct place in the AST representation of the code. I will try to fix it.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `RELEASING.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

Now let me look at the specific issue. Based on the problem description and the code I've analyzed, I can see the issue:

1. When formatting an enum class with a semicolon after the last enum entry, the `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule` adds a trailing comma before the semicolon
2. However, the code at lines 314-327 in `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt` has special handling for when `inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY`
3. This special handling removes the inspectNode (which is the semicolon) and adds it back as a child of the parent
4. The problem is that this manipulation happens AFTER the comma is added, which causes the AST to be in an inconsistent state

content.text:
I see! The `NoSemicolonsRule` checks if a semicolon is the last code leaf before the closing class body. If it is, then the semicolon is considered unnecessary and should be removed.

Now let me understand the issue better. The problem is:

1. When formatting an enum with a semicolon after the last enum entry, the `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule` detects that a trailing comma is missing
2. It adds the comma, but then it also manipulates the AST by removing the semicolon from the enum entry and adding it back to the parent
3. However, this manipulation happens AFTER the comma is added, which means the comma is added in the wrong location
4. The `NoSemicolonsRule` then runs and sees the semicolon as unnecessary (because it's now in the wrong location in the AST)

My fix moves the comma addition BEFORE the semicolon manipulation, which should fix the issue. However, I need to verify that this is the correct fix.

Let me think about this more carefully. Looking at the code:

```kotlin
if (inspectNode.treeParent.elementType == ElementType.ENUM_ENTRY) {
    with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {
        val parentIndent =
            (prevNode.psi.parent.prevLeaf() as? PsiWhiteSpace)?.text
                ?: "\n${prevNode.lineIndent()}"
        val newline = createWhiteSpace(parentIndent)
        val enumEntry = inspectNode.treeParent.psi
        enumEntry.apply {
            add(newline)
            removeChild(inspectNode)
            parent.addAfter(createSemicolon(), this)
        }
    }
}
```

This code is doing the following:
1. Creates a newline with the correct indentation
2. Adds the newline to the enum entry
3. Removes the semicolon (inspectNode) from the enum entry
4. Adds a new semicolon after the enum entry in the parent

The issue is that when the comma is added AFTER this manipulation, it's added to the wrong location. By moving the comma addition BEFORE this manipulation, the comma should be added in the correct location.

Good! There's only one place where enum entries are handled in this file, and I've already fixed it.

Now let me verify that the fix is complete by reviewing the entire flow:

1. When an enum with a semicolon after the last enum entry is formatted, the `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule` detects that a trailing comma is missing
2. The rule calls `reportAndCorrectTrailingCommaNodeBefore()` with the semicolon as the `inspectNode`
3. In the auto-correct section, the comma is now added BEFORE the semicolon manipulation
4. The semicolon is then removed from the enum entry and added back to the parent
5. The `NoSemicolonsRule` should now see the semicolon in the correct location and not report it as unnecessary

This should fix the issue described in the bug report.
